description:
You are acting as a release-manager who must register the next software build in an existing SQLite database and then hand the operations team a one-line confirmation file.

Environment already present:
1. Database file: /home/user/app_release.db  
2. Inside that database there is a table named “releases” created with the exact schema  
      CREATE TABLE releases(  
          id      INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,  
          version TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE,  
          status  TEXT    NOT NULL  
      );  

Goal:
1. Insert a new record into the table with  
      version = 'v1.2.3'  
      status  = 'ready'  
   (The id column must be allowed to auto-increment; do **not** hard-code it.)
2. After the insertion, generate a confirmation file at exactly  
      /home/user/release_log.csv  
   that contains **only one line** in comma-separated format (no spaces, no header line):  

      v1.2.3,ready  

   Requirements for /home/user/release_log.csv:  
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline “\n”.  
   • There must be no additional lines, spaces, or blank characters before or after the line.  
   • File permissions should remain default (0644) – no special changes are needed.

Constraints & Tips:
• Accomplish everything from an interactive shell using the standard sqlite3 CLI that is already installed.  
• Keep table headers OFF and output MODE set to csv when you generate the line that goes into the log file.  
• You only need to run one insertion and one select-export command in sqlite3; no other files should be modified or created.

The automated test will:
a) Open /home/user/app_release.db and verify that a row exists whose version='v1.2.3' and status='ready'.  
b) Read /home/user/release_log.csv and check that it contains exactly the single line “v1.2.3,ready\n”.

Return to the prompt once both the database change and the confirmation file are in place.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts:

1. File /home/user/app_release.db exists and is a valid SQLite database containing:
   • Table releases as defined in the task description.
   • Two rows (id=1, version='v1.0.0', status='deployed')  
                 (id=2, version='v1.1.0', status='deployed')

2. No file named /home/user/release_log.csv is present.

Expected state after successful task completion:

1. Database /home/user/app_release.db now contains a third row:
   id = 3            (any integer ≥3 is acceptable to the grader)  
   version = 'v1.2.3'  
   status  = 'ready'

2. File /home/user/release_log.csv exists with exact byte content:
   76 31 2E 32 2E 33 2C 72 65 61 64 79 0A
   (ASCII for “v1.2.3,ready\n”)

3. No other files were created or modified in /home/user.
